page: Google Cloud Platform h3-standard-88 is an x86_64 architecture server powered by an Intel Xeon 8481C processor operating at 2.7 GHz. It features 88 dedicated physical cores with a single thread per core, providing 88 vCPUs. The system is equipped with 352 GB of RAM, yielding a balanced memory-to-core ratio of 4.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or GPUs.

Benchmark data indicates top-tier multi-core CPU performance, with stress-ng multi-core and PassMark CPU scores placing in the top 10 percent. The processor's advanced instruction sets, including AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions, contribute to top-tier performance in extended instructions, encryption, and physics simulations. Memory bandwidth is also highly efficient, with read-only cached small block bandwidth exceeding 11,000 GB/sec and uncached large block read bandwidth reaching 469.37 GB/sec.

This server is highly suited for multi-threaded compute workloads, software compilation, database operations, and static web serving, all of which achieve top-tier benchmark results. It also demonstrates strong performance in LLM prompt processing. However, single-core performance for tasks like gzip compression and single-threaded bzip3 decompression remains in the average tier. The lack of local storage requires external network-attached storage, which should be factored into the overall architectural design.
description: Google Cloud Platform h3-standard-88 is an x86_64 server featuring 88 dedicated physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 8481C processor running at 2.7 GHz. With 352 GB of RAM, it maintains a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio. The architecture lacks local storage and GPUs but excels in multi-threaded compute tasks, achieving top-tier results in multi-core stress-ng, PassMark CPU, and database operations benchmarks. The processor supports advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and Intel AMX, which drive high performance in cryptography and physics simulations. While single-core performance is average, its massive memory bandwidth and high core density make it highly efficient for parallel processing, software compilation, static web serving, and medium-scale LLM inference prompt processing.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform h3-standard-88 is an x86_64 server with 88 dedicated Intel Xeon cores, 352 GB RAM, and top-tier multi-core performance, ideal for parallel computing and database workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform h3-standard-88 features 88 dedicated Intel Xeon cores and 352 GB RAM, delivering top-tier multi-core compute performance.
tagline: A high-density, dedicated-core x86 platform delivering exceptional multi-threaded compute performance and massive memory bandwidth for demanding enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 88 dedicated physical Intel Xeon cores with 1 thread per core
352 GB of system memory providing a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio
Top-tier multi-core CPU, database, and static web serving performance
Intel AMX and AVX-512 support for accelerated matrix and vector operations
No local storage or GPU resources included in the base configuration
Well-suited for software compilation, parallel processing, and database operations
